close_reason: Cannot fix: bitwarden SDK exposes org_id only through doc(hidden) .internal field. Upstream SDK change required.
description: src/lib.rs:182-189 reads the organization UUID via client.internal.get_access_token_organization() — the .internal field of bitwarden::Client is #[doc(hidden)] in bitwarden-core 2.0.0. The code's surrounding comment (lib.rs:166-181) accurately describes this as a private API and notes the upgrade-time check that needs to happen. This is a documented but unmitigated maintenance risk: any bitwarden 2.x point release can remove the field with no semver guarantee. Recommended actions: (1) File an upstream issue against RustCrypto-adjacent or Bitwarden's bitwarden-sdk repo asking for a stable public accessor on AccessTokenLoginResponse or Client (e.g. fn organization_id(&self) -> Option<Uuid>). Track the upstream issue ID here. (2) Add a compile-time pin to Cargo.toml: bitwarden = '=2.0.0' (exact version) until the upstream API stabilises, OR a runtime check that fails loudly if get_access_token_organization() returns None on a successful login (the current behavior is to map None to 'could not determine organization ID', which would be the user-visible symptom of an upstream API removal — but only after auth succeeds, masking the real cause). Adding a CI job that builds against the latest bitwarden minor release would catch removal earlier.
title: Crate depends on doc(hidden) bitwarden::Client::internal field for org ID
